source_basis.title: Hallmarks of aging: An expanding universe
source_basis.citation: Lopez-Otin C, Blasco MA, Partridge L, Serrano M, Kroemer G. Cell. 2023;186(2):243-278.

hallmarks.name: Genomic Instability
hallmarks.description: Accumulation of genetic damage and impaired genome maintenance.

hallmarks.name: Telomere Attrition
hallmarks.description: Progressive telomere shortening or dysfunction that limits cell renewal.

hallmarks.name: Epigenetic Alterations
hallmarks.description: Age-related drift in DNA methylation, chromatin state, and transcriptional regulation.

hallmarks.name: Loss of Proteostasis
hallmarks.description: Decline in protein quality control, folding, trafficking, and clearance.

hallmarks.name: Disabled Macroautophagy
hallmarks.description: Reduced autophagic recycling that impairs cellular housekeeping and stress response.

hallmarks.name: Deregulated Nutrient Sensing
hallmarks.description: Age-related disruption of insulin, IGF-1, mTOR, AMPK, and related nutrient signaling pathways.

hallmarks.name: Mitochondrial Dysfunction
hallmarks.description: Decline in mitochondrial quality, energetics, and signaling.

hallmarks.name: Cellular Senescence
hallmarks.description: Accumulation of damaged cells with stable cell-cycle arrest and pro-inflammatory signaling.

hallmarks.name: Stem Cell Exhaustion
hallmarks.description: Loss of regenerative capacity from impaired stem and progenitor cell function.

hallmarks.name: Altered Intercellular Communication
hallmarks.description: Age-related distortion of signaling between cells, tissues, and organs.

hallmarks.name: Chronic Inflammation
hallmarks.description: Persistent, maladaptive inflammatory tone that contributes to systemic aging.

hallmarks.name: Dysbiosis
hallmarks.description: Age-related disruption of host-microbiome balance with downstream metabolic and immune effects.
